meta.title: Address Delivered at the Dedication of the Cemetery at Gettysburg

lexical_units.basic_meaning: bringing a child or living being into existence
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political founding is understood through human generation.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The nation is not literally born, yet its founding is framed through birth-generation language.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: political founding of a nation is represented through generation or birth
lexical_units.review_notes: Gettysburg opening uses birth-generation language; preserve the period-control note on conceived/brought forth.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: bringing a child or living being into existence
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political founding is understood through human generation.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The nation is not literally born, yet its founding is framed through birth-generation language.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: political founding of a nation is represented through generation or birth
lexical_units.review_notes: Gettysburg opening uses birth-generation language; preserve the period-control note on conceived/brought forth.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: bringing a child or living being into existence
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political founding is understood through human generation.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The nation is not literally born, yet its founding is framed through birth-generation language.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: political founding of a nation is represented through generation or birth
lexical_units.review_notes: Gettysburg opening uses birth-generation language; preserve the period-control note on conceived/brought forth.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: set apart or devote to a sacred, solemn, or special purpose
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political commitment is understood through dedication or consecration.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: A political community or task is not physically set apart, but is framed as solemnly devoted.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: committed the nation or people to a political principle
lexical_units.review_notes: Ceremonial and religious-register force is plausible; keep distinct from later CMT mapping.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a living organism continues alive, dies, perishes, survives, or endures bodily strain
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political survival is understood through organismic life and death.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: A polity is not a biological organism, but its political existence is construed as bodily life or death.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: the nation or government continues or ceases political existence
lexical_units.review_notes: Body-politic survival language; use as MIPVU evidence only with its local national subject.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: physical rest after activity or sleep-like repose
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Death or burial is understood through bodily rest/repose.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The contextual meaning concerns burial/death rather than ordinary bodily rest after exertion.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: burial or cemetery repose for the war dead
lexical_units.review_notes: Conventional death-as-rest euphemism in a cemetery dedication setting; retain as metaphor-related but not high-confidence ideological evidence by itself.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: transfer possession of something to another
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Sacrificial death is understood through transfer or offering of a valued possession.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Lives are not ordinary transferable objects, but the verb frames death as a gift or offering.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: surrendered or offered their lives in battle
lexical_units.review_notes: Supports later sacrificial-body review when linked with lives/nation/live context; avoid treating the verb alone as sufficient evidence.

lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political survival is understood by comparison with organismic life.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: A nation is not a biological organism, but its survival is construed as life.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: the nation would continue to exist politically
lexical_units.review_notes: Strong candidate for NATION IS LIVING BODY/ORGANISM mapping in this sentence.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a person noting, remembering, or forgetting
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Collective historical memory is understood through human cognition.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The world is treated as a remembering person.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: public memory is attributed to the world as an agent
lexical_units.review_notes: Reliability-sample sentence; likely personification rather than merely literal global public.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: set apart or devote to a sacred, solemn, or special purpose
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political obligation is understood through dedication or consecrated devotion.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: The living are not physically set apart like an object or place; they are morally committed to a public task.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: committed to public work or obligation
lexical_units.review_notes: Ceremonial register may make this conventional; preserve rival reading during CMT mapping.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: transfer possession of something to another
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Sacrificial commitment is understood as an offering or gift.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Devotion and death are framed as something given, not as an ordinary transferable object.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: sacrificed or expended themselves for the cause
lexical_units.review_notes: Part of phrase 'gave the last full measure of devotion'; map only with surrounding units.

lexical_units.comparison_basis: Moral commitment is understood through measurable quantity or fullness.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Devotion is abstract and not literally measured as a physical quantity.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: complete extent of devotion or sacrifice
lexical_units.review_notes: Works with 'last full measure of devotion' as moral accounting/sacrificial scale.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: emergence of a child or living being from the womb
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political renewal is understood through generation and birth.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Freedom is not biologically born, but political renewal is explicitly described as birth.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: political renewal of freedom after war sacrifice
lexical_units.review_notes: High-salience candidate for POLITICAL RENEWAL IS BIRTH; check against historical semantics note on birth/conceived/brought forth.

lexical_units.basic_meaning: a living being dies or is destroyed
lexical_units.comparison_basis: Political survival is understood through organismic life and death.
lexical_units.contrast_explanation: Government is not a biological organism, but its failure is construed as death/perishing.
lexical_units.contextual_meaning: government would not disappear or fail politically
lexical_units.review_notes: Complements nation/live and nation/birth language in the same address.
